data.title: Trump administration explores taking stakes in more critical minerals companies - report
data.description:
The Trump administration is in talks to take equity stakes in multiple critical minerals companies, The Information reported Thursday, the latest in an unprecedented wave of dealmaking that could give the U.S. government stakes in several companies.

The negotiations largely involve Department of Energy grants and loans for companies that mine and process the metals and components used in batteries, according to the report, which cited consultants with clients in the talks.

The talks follow the July deal where the U.S. government received a 15% stake in rare earths producer MP Materials (NYSE:MP) in exchange for $400 million; MP shares +11.6% to as high as $82.23 in Thursday's trading, near an all-time intraday high.

Among other potentially relevant stocks: Aclara Resources (OTCPK:ARAAF) +18.2%, NioCorp Developments (NB) +11.7%, Ucore Rare Metals (OTCQX:UURAF) +9.7%, USA Rare Earth (USAR) +8.8%, Critical Metals (CRML) +8.3%, TMC the metals company (TMC) +6.5%, Ramaco Resources (METC) +3.1%, Rare Element Resources (OTCQB:REEMF) +1.5%, Lynas Rare Earths (OTCPK:LYSCF) +1.3%.

Lithium Americas shares nearly doubled on Wednesday following reports that the U.S. government may receive up to a 10% stake in the company.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:REMX), (LIT), (BATT), (URA), (NLR), (URNM)

data.title: CarMax outlines $150M SG&A reduction target while emphasizing nimble pricing strategy
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: CarMax, Inc. (KMX) Q2 2026

Management View

  • CEO William Nash opened by acknowledging that "our second quarter results fell short of our expectations," but emphasized the company remains focused on "driving sales, gaining market share and delivering significant year-over-year earnings growth for years to come." He detailed four key priorities: maintaining competitive pricing and selection, increasing consumer awareness with the new "Wanna Drive?" campaign, achieving at least $150 million in SG&A reductions over the next 18 months, and increasing profit from diversified business components, especially CarMax Auto Finance (CAF) penetration and profitability.
  • Nash elaborated that "during the quarter, we delivered total sales of $6.6 billion, down 6% compared to last year, reflecting lower volume." He noted a decline in retail and wholesale unit sales, with average retail selling price at $26,000 and a sequential drop in retail gross profit per used unit.
  • CFO Enrique Mayor-Mora stated, "Second quarter net earnings per diluted share was $0.64 versus $0.85 a year ago. The decrease was driven primarily by lower volume and the CAF loss provision adjustment." He added, "Total gross profit was $718 million, down 6% from last year's second quarter."
  • Jon Daniels, Senior Vice President, highlighted that CAF originated over $2 billion, achieving a sales penetration of 42.6%. He noted, "CAF income for the quarter was $103 million, down $13 million from FY '25. Net interest margin on the portfolio was 6.6%, up over 50 basis points from last year and relatively in line with last quarter."

Outlook

  • Management committed to delivering incremental SG&A reductions of at least $150 million over the next 18 months, with Mayor-Mora stating, "this does not impact our growth strategy as we will continue to invest in initiatives that position us for the future."
  • The company expects the majority of SG&A savings to materialize by the end of fiscal 2027. Mayor-Mora highlighted, "While we expect to realize some of these savings this fiscal year, we expect the vast majority will materialize in our exit rate by the end of fiscal 2027."
  • Increased marketing spend is anticipated in the back half of the year to support the new brand campaign. Management projects positive service margin for the full year, despite expected seasonal pressure.

Financial Results

  • CarMax reported total sales of $6.6 billion, a year-over-year decline of 6%. Retail unit sales fell 5.4%, and used unit comps were down 6.3%. Retail gross profit per used unit was $2,216, with wholesale gross profit per unit at $993.
  • The company bought approximately 293,000 vehicles during the quarter, with consumer purchases accounting for 262,000. CAF reported a provision of $142 million, with a reserve balance of $507 million, and noted additional losses anticipated from 2022 and 2023 loan vintages.
  • SG&A expenses were $601 million, down 2% year-over-year. Mayor-Mora cited continued efficiency improvements, including the deployment of AI technology such as Skye 2.0 to drive operational gains.
  • CarMax repurchased approximately 2.9 million shares for $180 million during the quarter, with $1.56 billion remaining on the repurchase authorization.

Q&A

  • Brian Nagel, Oppenheimer: Asked about sales disruptions and whether business has normalized post Q2. Nash responded that "each month was down year-over-year, and each month got a little weaker throughout the quarter. Now what I'll tell you for September and month-to-date is that it is stronger than the quarter and any of the months in the second quarter."
  • Rajat Gupta, JPMorgan: Queried about CAF income outlook and provision changes. Daniels responded, "we did highlight that we thought there would be increase year-over-year in CAF income. I think we're going to be flat to down...but I think there's some nice trade-offs that are occurring there."
  • Sharon Zackfia, William Blair: Asked if SG&A savings will be reinvested to drive sales. Nash replied, "some of that we will reinvest directly back into things of driving sales."
  • Christopher Bottiglieri, BNP Paribas: Asked about subprime lending. Nash clarified, "we're not talking about deep subprime," and Daniels added, "we're being very prudent...there is money to be made there."
  • David Bellinger, Mizuho: Asked about consumer health and market competitiveness. Nash responded that "consumers, especially your mid to high FICO customers, they seem to be sitting on the sidelines a little bit."
  • Michael Montani, Evercore ISI: Queried about credit trends and provisions. Daniels stated, "I think the way you should think about it is the provision this quarter for the new originations...is pretty representative."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concerns about sustained sales weakness, CAF provision volatility, and whether SG&A reductions might impact growth. Their tone was neutral to slightly negative, seeking clarity on strategy execution and risk.
  • Management maintained a confident but cautious tone in prepared remarks, with Nash stating, "we are confident in our strategy and our earnings model." In Q&A, management was transparent about challenges and more defensive when pressed on pricing and credit risk, emphasizing flexibility and ongoing cost control.
  • Compared to last quarter, the tone shifted from positive and growth-focused to more cautious, with analysts pressing harder on operational risks and management highlighting mitigation strategies and cost discipline.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q1 featured strong retail comps, record retail gross profit per used unit, and robust EPS growth, supported by volume gains and omni-channel efficiencies. In Q2, sales and unit volumes declined, retail margins compressed, and CAF income fell, with notable increases in loan loss provisions.
  • Last quarter, management was focused on growth and leveraging SG&A; in Q2, attention shifted to cost containment and operational efficiency, with an explicit $150 million SG&A reduction target. Guidance language became more cautious, reflecting market headwinds and consumer challenges.
  • Analysts' focus shifted from growth and digital initiatives to pricing competitiveness, CAF risk, and the sustainability of cost reductions.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted lingering macroeconomic headwinds, inventory depreciation, and continued pressure on mid-to-high FICO consumer demand.
  • Analysts questioned the volatility in CAF provisions, impact of aggressive pricing, and whether cost-cutting could constrain market share recovery.
  • Management described mitigation strategies including nimble pricing, continued investment in technology, and prudent credit expansion.

Final Takeaway

Management emphasized that while second quarter results did not meet expectations, the company is taking decisive steps to navigate the current environment by focusing on operational efficiency, competitive pricing, and targeted cost reductions. The commitment to at least $150 million in SG&A reductions over the next 18 months, ongoing investment in technology, and a flexible approach to pricing and inventory position CarMax to pursue market share growth and long-term earnings improvement despite near-term challenges.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Google DeepMind unveils new AI models that use the web to aid robots
data.description:
Google DeepMind (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) unveiled two new artificial intelligence models on Thursday that the tech company says will allow robots to utilize the internet to perform certain tasks. 

The models, known as Gemini Robotics 1.5 and Gemini Robotics-ER 1.5, will allow the robots to think, plan and make logical decisions inside their physical environments.

The Gemini Robotics 1.5 model — which is a vision-language-action model — “thinks before taking action and shows its process, helping robots assess and complete complex tasks more transparently,” Google DeepMind said in a blog post. “It also learns across embodiments, accelerating skill learning.”

Building on that, the Gemini Robotics-ER 1.5 model — which is a vision-language model — “reasons about the physical world, natively calls digital tools and creates detailed, multi-step plans to complete a mission.”

Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai added that the new models will help robots transfer learning from one kind of robot to another; he also said it is Alphabet's “next big step towards general-purpose robots that are truly helpful.”

The tech giant provided an example if a robot was asked if it could sort objects into the correct recycling and trash bins, based on its location. The Gemini Robotics-ER 1.5 model would bring a robot's activities together, like a brain, and help perform tasks such as looking up Google Search to look for information and other third-party tools to come up with an answer.

“These advances will help developers build more capable and versatile robots that can actively understand their environment to complete complex, multi-step tasks in a general way,” Google DeepMind added.

The Gemini Robotics-ER 1.5 model is available to developers via the Gemini API in Google AI Studio, while the Gemini Robotics 1.5 model is already available to select partners.

data.title: Mortgage rates inch up after several weeks of decline
data.description:
Mortgage rates inched up after several weeks of decline, but the housing market activity remained solid, according to the latest Freddie Mac Primary Mortgage Survey.

30-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 6.30% as of September 25, up from 6.26% last week and 6.08% in the year-ago week.

15-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 5.49%, compared to 5.41% in the prior week and 5.16% a year ago.

"Housing market activity continues to hold up with purchase and refinance applications increasing by 18% and 42%, respectively, compared to the same time last year," said Sam Khater, Freddie Mac's chief economist.

data.title: Amazon fined $2.5B after FTC proves company illegally enrolled members onto Prime
data.description:
E-commerce and tech giant Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) will pay $2.5B in penalties to settle allegations brought by the Federal Trade Commission after the company signed up millions of customers for Prime subscriptions without their permission and also made it difficult to cancel them.

The U.S. government agency said Thursday Amazon will pay $1B in civil penalties, refund $1.5B to customers who were deceived by the company, and cease unlawful enrollment and cancellation practices for Prime.

"The evidence showed that Amazon used sophisticated subscription traps designed to manipulate consumers into enrolling in Prime, and then made it exceedingly hard for consumers to end their subscription," FTC Chairman Andrew Ferguson said in a public statement.

The FTC has ordered the company to make clear disclosures about all material terms of a Prime membership during enrollment and have a third-party supervisor to monitor the company's compliance with the consumer redress distribution process.

The regulator has also ordered the company to remove its "No, I don’t want Free Shipping" button and introduce a new button for customers to decline Prime membership easily.

data.title: The housing market may see a turnaround despite current struggles – UBS
data.description:
A bottom is in sight for the housing market, according to John Lovallo, UBS homebuilders and building products analyst, who is predicting significant improvement for 2026 despite current underperformance.

Real estate (XLRE), (NYSEARCA:IYR), (VNQ) remains one of the worst-performing sectors this year, with homebuilders (XHB) across the board in correction territory, falling at least 10% from their 52-week highs. As an example, KB Home (KBH) recently reduced its full-year sales guidance due to lower prices, indicating the market remains challenged, even when the company delivered strong quarterly results, beating expectations on all key performance indicators.

“A bottom is in sight, and 2026 is set up to be a much better year for housing than 2025,” Lovallo said in an interview with CNBC.

He said he sees “stabilization across the broad housing market and particularly in markets that are very important like Florida and Texas.”

Mortgage rates remain a critical factor affecting homebuilder performance. “What we need is rate stabilization. These builders are having to put in an incredible amount of incentives into the market, which lowers the ASP (the average selling price) in order to stimulate demand,” Lovallo said.

He added that if rates stabilize, buyers will “have a better idea of what they’re paying” and “feel more confident in the overall environment.”

The analyst explained the difference in pricing strategies among builders, pointing out that some companies like KB Home (KBH) tend to reduce home prices directly rather than using mortgage rate buy-downs.

“Every 100 basis points of buy down is about 350 basis points of gross margin,” he noted, suggesting that price stabilization could significantly boost profitability. This becomes “very powerful” if mortgage rates eventually decline.

Lovallo sees approximately 30% upside potential for KB Home (KBH) shares even if rates remain at current levels.

His top pick in the sector, however, is Meritage Homes (MTH), with a particular focus on entry-level builders.

“We like the entry level the best. We like D.R. Horton (DHI) a lot, but really we’re bullish across the group,” he said, adding that opportunities for builders are “getting increasingly powerful as we move through the year.”

Earlier, the National Association of Realtors reported that U.S. existing home sales dipped 0.2% month-over-month, but lower than expected.

data.title: Stitch Fix surrenders post-earnings gains as shrinking user base, compressed margins spook investors
data.description:
Shares of Stitch Fix (NASDAQ:SFIX) have surrendered after-hour gains and are now deep in negative territory as investors take profits after digesting remarks from the company’s earning call after the close on Wednesday.

The stock was briefly launched higher on better-than-expected fiscal first quarter results and guidance that expects a 4.4% to 6% increase in sales.

But while results overshadowed a 15th consecutive quarter of subscriber loss, the FY26 EBITDA outlook forecasts a modest deterioration from the year prior as the company’s admission of “external headwinds” is likely to blame.

According to Mizuho’s David Bellinger, the revenue growth of as much as 6% is accompanied by weakening gross margin rates and advertising spending that is reaching 10% of revenue.

“We believe senior management is pushing SFIX in the right direction off a low base yet see challenges ahead in returning to sustained growth and harvesting EBITDA margins well above the current +LSD% trajectory,” he says.

During the company’s earnings call, CEO Matt Baer and CFO David Aufderhaar were both upbeat regarding the company’s results, highlighting new AI tools, improved inventory, and deepening the relationship between clients and stylists.

But CFO Aufderhaar warned that the company is entering the holiday season with a “challenging macro environment,” and a narrower profit margin due to higher transportation costs and “ongoing strategic investments in our client experience and assortment.”

While his remarks were not overly alarming, the spike in shares after quarterly results (to the highest level this year) coupled with a shrinking user base and modest FY26 guidance likely fueled profit-taking.

Stitch Fix (NASDAQ:SFIX) shares were down as much as 18% on Thursday from Wedesday's close and down more than 20% from the afterhours high.

data.title: Marvell rises after top executives buy shares of chipmaker
data.description:
Shares of Marvell Technology (NASDAQ:MRVL) rose about 3% on Thursday after regulatory filings showed that several company executives bought the chipmaker's stock.

Chairman and CEO Matt Murphy bought 13,600 shares at $77 apiece. Following this, Murphy owns 268,637 shares of the company.

Chris Koopmans, president and chief operating officer, acquired 6,800 shares at $78.03 per share and now owns 104,825 shares of Marvell.

The company's CFO Willem Meintjes bought 3,400 shares at $78.03 apiece. Meintjes now has 132,159 shares in total.

Sandeep Bharathi, President of the Data Center Group at Marvell, acquired 3,400 shares at $78.03 per share and now owns 73,392 shares of Marvell.

data.title: xAI's Grok receives clearance for use across federal agencies; Musk sues OpenAI over trade secrets
data.description:
xAI, which is run by Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) CEO Elon Musk, has received approval for some of its Grok artificial intelligence models to be used by federal government employees, according to the U.S. General Services Administration.

Federal agencies can gain access to Grok 4 and Grok 4 Fast for $0.42 per organization for the next 18 months.

"Widespread access to advanced AI models is essential to building the efficient, accountable government that taxpayers deserve—and to fulfilling President Trump's promise that America will win the global AI race," said Federal Acquisition Service Commissioner Josh Gruenbaum.

"Thanks to President Trump and his administration, xAI's frontier AI is now unlocked for every federal agency empowering the U.S. Government to innovate faster and accomplish its mission more effectively than ever before," said Musk, xAI co-founder and CEO. "We look forward to continuing to work with President Trump and his team to rapidly deploy AI throughout the government for the benefit of the country."

The deal is even cheaper than other AI models that have received approval this summer to be used by the federal government. Microsoft (MSFT)-backed OpenAI is providing access to ChatGPT Enterprise to U.S. federal agencies at a nominal cost of $1 per agency for the next year. The GSA has also added AI services from Anthropic's Claude and Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Gemini.

xAI sues OpenAI

Musk's xAI has also filed a lawsuit against OpenAI. The civil suit alleges former xAI employees stole company secrets related to Grok and provided them to OpenAI.

"The desire to win the artificial intelligence race has driven OpenAI to cross the line of fair play," reads the lawsuit, which was filed in the U.S. District Court for Northern California on Wednesday, Sept. 24. "OpenAI violated California and federal law by inducing former xAI employees, including Xuechen Li, Jimmy Fraiture, and a senior finance executive, to steal and share xAI's trade secrets. By hook or by crook, OpenAI clearly will do anything when threatened by a better innovator, including plundering and misappropriating the technical advancements, source code, and business plans of xAI."

data.title: DMG Blockchain lands Buy rating from Canaccord
data.description:
Canaccord Genuity recently initiated coverage on DMG Blockchain Solutions (OTCQB:DMGGF) (TSXV:DMGI:CA) with a Buy rating, owing to the digital infrastructure company's "triple growth engine."

Analyst Tania Armstrong-Whitworth talked up DMG's (OTCQB:DMGGF) diversified, "balanced approach," with exposure to both bitcoin (BTC-USD) mining and software services.

"We believe this balanced approach positions DMGI as one of the more technically sophisticated and disciplined operators in the North American landscape," the analyst wrote in a note.

DMGGF shares, changing hands at $0.24 a share, dipped 6.6% in Thursday late morning trading.
